name: MUCINOUS CYSTIC NEOPLASM OF PANCREAS
definition: MACROCYSTIC ADENOMA OF PANCREAS = MUCINOUS CYSTADENOMA thick-walled uni- / multilocular low-grade malignant tumor composed of large mucin-

demographics.Path: large smooth round / lobulated multiloculated cystic mass encapsulated by a layer of
demographics.Histo: cysts lined by tall columnar, mucin-producing cells subtended by a densely cellular
demographics.Location: often in pancreatic tail (90%) / body, head (rare)

dominant_finding: fine internal septa + small intramural nodules may not be visible ± contrast enhancement →
distribution: often in pancreatic tail (90%) / body, head (rare)
discriminating_features: well-demarcated thick-walled mass with < 2 mm thin septa
solid papillary excrescences protrude into the interior of tumor (= sign of malignancy)
± vascular encasement and splenic vein occlusion

general: well-demarcated thick-walled mass with < 2 mm thin septa

clinical.Prognosis: invariably transformation into cystadenocarcinoma; 17-63% 5-year survival rate
clinical.Rx: complete surgical excision (5-year survival rate of 74-90%)
